channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Steve Jackson Games Announces Seven-Part Pact, A TTRPG About Wizards and Binding Promises
item.content:encoded:
Steve Jackson Games has a new TTRPG coming to Kickstarter. This new game, called Seven-Part Pact, is a game about being one of the most powerful wizards in the universe, with all of the magical might, and complex responsibilities, that that title entails.

The Seven-Part Pact Announcement

In its official press release, Seven-Part Pact is a game designed for three to seven players. When the game starts, each player has become one of the most powerful wizards in the world. But with this unfathomable power comes a great price. These almighty spellslingers feel lonely and incarcerated, bound by an venerable pact.

Each of these wizards have a choice. Since they control one of seven parts of the known world, will they live their lives unchanged...or annihilate the world as it is known.

Every player in Seven-Part Pact is in charge of one of seven books to base their character's backstories and roles in the Celestial Audience on as they face crucial challenges. These wizards also share a spellbook, called a Grimoire, which can be used to cast spells and change the game's rules. 

This elaborate TTRPG also has euro board game elements. Each wizard plays through the game in month-long segments. Over this time, they will consult the stars and planets, using their placement to control their Domain. This control manifests in arcane secrets over the span of months and years, secrets that can rewrite the fundamental laws of the world.

Seven-Part Pact is the latest project by Jay Dragon. Dragon's body of work includes the award-winning pastoral fantasy RPG, Wanderhome, and the queer slice-of-life board game, Yazeba's Bed & Breakfast.

At the time of writing, further details on Seven-Part Pact are limited. The Kickstarter campaign mentions the game will contain multiple asymettric board game elements, but its mum on other elements like dice and gameplay mechanics. However, the fact that game allows its fundamental rules to be changed is reminiscent of the award-winning mindbending TTRPG, Triangle Agency.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler
